page_content:
Formerly: BUAC758V.

BUAC765 Business Ethics for Accountants and Auditors (3 Credits) Considers all facets of business ethics issues within an accounting and auditing context. Ethical theory, corporate social responsibility,

and individual decision-making are considered. Some of the applied topics that may be covered in this course include, but are not limited to, intellectual property issues, corporate downsizing, outsourcing, global ethics, crises management, and employment ethics.

Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program. Credit Only Granted for. BUAC765 or BUAC706.

BUAC766 Data Security and Taxation of Not-for-Profit Entities (2 Credits) An introduction to the theory and practice of taxation as applied in

the nonprofit sector. The practice of taxation for nonprofits requires,

inter alia, an understanding of the legal framework for these entities. Therefore, the course will devote significant time to understanding the legal environment and framework associated with nonprofits. We will also discuss the related issues of data security and cybersecurity. The course goal is to enable students to become proficient in the major aspects of nonprofits that apply to the work of public accountants.

Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H Smith School of Business.
